fundamentals.name: Starbucks Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Starbucks Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, operates as a roaster, marketer, and retailer of coffee internationally. The company operates through three segments: North America, International, and Channel Development. Its stores offer coffee, tea, and other beverages, roasted whole beans and ground coffees, complementary food, packaged coffees, single-serve products, and ready-to-drink beverages; and various food products, such as pastries, breakfast sandwiches, and lunch items. The company also licenses its trademarks through licensed stores, and grocery and foodservice accounts. The company offers its products under the Starbucks Coffee, Teavana, Seattle's Best Coffee, Ethos, and Starbucks Reserve brands. Starbucks Corporation was founded in 1971 and is based in Seattle, Washington.

judgment.summary: A company with strong analyst sentiment and recent earnings beats, but facing headwinds from slowing growth, high valuation, and negative free cash flow.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 14.5%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Revenue growth 8.8% YoY, with quarterly earnings growth at 33.0%.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $106.58.
Last quarter's EPS beat estimates by 14.5%.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 77.55 and Forward P/E of 33.54 indicate a high valuation.
Free cash flow is negative at $-1.30B TTM.
Dividend payout ratio is unsustainably high at 187.8%.
Operating margin of 8.4% and profit margin of 3.9% are relatively low.
Current ratio of 0.92 and quick ratio of 0.26 suggest potential liquidity issues.

news.paragraph: Starbucks is facing headwinds, with its stock down 3.2% since its last earnings report, which saw an EPS beat. The company recently pulled the plug on an AI inventory program across North American stores, and its CEO has sent a blunt message regarding consumer spending. Despite a 'buy' consensus from analysts and a recent 'Bull of the Day' mention, the operational shifts and consumer sentiment concerns weigh on the stock.

headlines.title: Why Is Starbucks (SBUX) Down 3.2% Since Last Earnings Report?
Reuters: Starbucks Ends AI Inventory Program across North American Stores
Starbucks CEO sends blunt message on consumer spending
Arm shares extend weekly rally to almost 50%, and Starbucks pulls the plug on an AI project
